taxonomy: Tectaria trichodes is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of tropical fern that belongs to the genus Tectaria. The classification of Tectaria trichodes places it in the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. intro: Tectaria trichodes, also known as Indian fan fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Tectaria trichodes contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, boost the immune system, and promote overall well-being.
